item.title: Lott — Love and Theft
item.description: Author: Eric Lott Title: Love and Theft: Blackface Minstrelsy and the American Working Class Year: 1993 Type: Academic book (Oxford University Press) A study of blackface minstrelsy that reframes white America’s relationship with Black culture as simultaneously one of desire and domination.

item.title: Miller — Segregating Sound
item.description: Author: Karl Hagstrom Miller Title: Segregating Sound: Inventing Folk and Pop Music in the Age of Jim Crow Year: 2010 Type: Academic book (Duke University Press) A revisionist history of American music’s racial categories that argues the distinction between “Black music” and “white music” was an ind...

item.title: Toynbee — Making Popular Music
item.description: Author: Jason Toynbee Title: Making Popular Music: Musicians, Creativity and Institutions Year: 2000 Publisher: Arnold (London) / Oxford University Press (New York) Type: Book Toynbee’s target is the Romantic ideal of the solitary genius producing original work from inner inspiration — and his claim...

item.title: Warwick — Girl Groups, Girl Culture
item.description: Author: Jacqueline Warwick Title: Girl Groups, Girl Culture: Popular Music and Identity in the 1960s Year: 2007 Type: Academic book (Routledge Studies in Popular Music) Warwick takes the records the rock canon filed under “disposable teen pop” (the Brill Building product, written by men, run by men)...

item.title: Wilder — American Popular Song
item.description: Author: Alec Wilder Title: American Popular Song: The Great Innovators, 1900–1950 Year: 1972 Publisher: Oxford University Press Type: Book (edited and with an introduction by James T.

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Genres: folk rock, contemporary folk, singer-songwriter, blues rock, country rock Scene: Greenwich Village folk scene Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an ac...

item.title: Chicago blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → Chicago blues Period: Late 1940s–present (peak: 1948–mid-1960s) Location: Chicago, Illinois (South Side and West Side) Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues RYM Muddy Waters’s slide guitar cutting through the noise of a South Side club at two in the morning,...

item.title: Electric blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues Period: 1938–present (peak: late 1940s–late 1960s) Location: Chicago, Memphis, Texas, Los Angeles Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues, gospel, jazz RYM A guitar string bent through a cheap amplifier in a loud club on Chicago’s South Side, the note hanging in...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: Blues → Jump blues Period: Late 1930s–mid-1950s Location: Kansas City, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago Key influences: Swing, big band, boogie woogie, blues RYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancin...
